Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Elena's escalation using search_emails or conversations_search_messages. The model should discover email from elena.rostova@moveops.com with subject "URGENT: Meridian Consulting escalation - immediate attention needed". It is mentioned in the email that the client is a $500K+ client and David should consider this his top priority. It's also mentioned that Julian brooks has the technical details.
2. Search emails for other open client issues using search_emails or list_emails to review David's inbox. This should lead to the model discovering four additional emails. One of the emails is from Mina Hashimoto about CloudTech onboarding docs review, one if from Julian Brooks about TechGrow reporting dashboard access blocked by a permission issue on MoveOp's end, needing David's approval on temporary elevated access and engineering escalation, another is from Chloe Vance about Acme corp relocation timeline (operation stuck, need confirmation on April 15 move date, apartment preferences and flight booking and the fourth email is from Emeka Diallo about Phoenix Industries wanting to restructure their relocation package across 3 move dates, pending David's approval in Linear issue OPS-847, client expecting confirmation by Friday).
3. Search slack messages for urgent client issues or mentions of David using conversations_search_messages (query: "urgent" or "david" or "escalation" or "bug"). The model should discover Julian Brooks in customer-support reporting TechVision platform bug, Mina Hashimoto in customer-engagement reminding David about CloudTech onboarding docs review, Emeka Diallo in customer-engagement reminding David about Phoenix Industries restructuring approval in Linear. Also, the TechVision platform bug is a critical finding and may be connected to Meridian's conflicting dates issue since both involve platform side display or data problems.
4. Search Linear for existing tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "urgent" or "TechVision" or "TechGrow" or "Phoenix" or "Meridian"). The model should discover three existing issues, one for Phoenix Industries account restructuring approval (issue 847, priority 2, assigned to moveops_david_chen), second for TechVision dashboard search filter bug (issue 484, priority 1, unassigned), and the third for TechGrow account permission issue (dashboard access blocked, issue 485, priority 2, unassigned).
5. Check David's upcoming calendar events using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-26", end_datetime: "2026-04-29") or calendar_search_events (query: "David" or "client"). The model should discover two upcoming meetings. One meeting titled "URGENT: Meridian Consulting escalation call" with 3 attendees, namely, David, Elena and Julian Brooks, and description "Emergency call to address Meridian's date conflict issue. Discuss platform configuration audit and remediation options. $500K account at risk." Another meeting is titled "Customer Support handoff: TechGrow + TechVision issues", the attendees are David, Julian Brooks and Zara Kovacevic, and description "Review TechGrow permission dashboard issue, TechVision platform bug, and approve escalation path to Engineering. Discuss SLA impacts."
6. Look up Elena Rostova's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query:"Elena" or "Rostova" or "Elena Rostova") to confirm her email is elena.rostova@moveops.com for sending the breakdown email.
7. List slack channels using channels_list to identify the customer engagement channel ID (C002, #customer-engagement) for posting the summary where Mina and Emeka will see it.
8. Create a tracking ticket for the Meridian Consulting escalation using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations", title: "URGENT: Meridian Consulting client escalation - conflicting dates and overbooked flights" or similar, priority: 1, description covering the $500K account, conflicting platform dates, overbooked flights, need for configuration audit and bug vs config determination). This is the highest priority issue.
9. Create a tracking ticket for the Acme Corp relocation timeline using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations", title: "Acme Corp relocation timelines - blocking operations" or similar, priority: 2, dsescription covering the April 15 move date uncertainty, apartment sourcing blocked, flight booking hold decision needed). This is an urgent untracked issue.
10. Send email to Elena Rostova using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com"], subject: "Client issue breakdown and remediation plan" or something similar). The email should contain a clear breakdown of every critical client issue discovered, like Meridian consulting's issue of conflicting dates, overbooked flights, and it's a $500K account so something about how important the account is, the need to figuring out if platform bug or configuration error is the issue and that Julian has the technical details and so on. Similarly for TechVision, which has a platform bug with search filters on relocation dashboard, multiple customers have been affected, Zara is troubleshooting, engineering escalation is needed and that it might be related to Meridian's date conflicts. Then we have TechGrow, with its dashboard permission issues, Acme corp has its operations blocked, Phoenix Industries has restructuring request for 3 move dates pending David's approval and so on. And CloudTech has onboarding blocked . Each of the issue should have a clear what went wrong and a plan for resolution.
11. Post summary in the #customer-engagement channel using conversations_add_message. The message should cover the key issues.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
